verbatim_text: And just to emphasize this once again, if we look at what these attention diagrams actually look like in practice, they look very tree like. Okay, so to summarize our findings, language models do build and use internal world models. And they perform this sort of associative scan algorithm to track and update world state. So why does this matter? What real world problems does this help us solve? I don't have time to go through all these works today, but I've also worked on improving language model coherence and updatability by improving their world models through training objectives, targeted internal edits, and updating retrieval based memories. Okay, so now we're going to turn to user models.
